dataset_title: Portfolio Investment Positions by Counterpart Economy (formerly Coordinated Portfolio Investment Survey, CPIS)

reopened.reason: DP1-reopen. Prior DP2 verdict tagged CPIS bilateral as a HOLE / one-dense-column-only. build/audit/cpis_probe.json proved that was an artifact of a wrong reporter code: CL_PIP_COUNTRY uses ISO-3 ('USA'), not 2-letter 'US'; the SDMX API returns HTTP 200 + EMPTY for a syntactically-valid-but-nonexistent member, so the wrong code masqueraded as 'no data'. This contract re-grounds the source live and runs a coverage census.
reopened.supersedes_bug: Prior contract's data example used key 'US.A.P_TOTINV_P_USD....' (reporter='US') which returns HTTP 200 / 0 obs. Corrected reporter member is 'USA'.

role_in_matrix: Bilateral portfolio-investment positions (equity + investment fund shares + debt securities) between economies, by counterpart (issuer) economy and by holder x issuer institutional sector. Supplies the portfolio-investment from-whom-to-whom layer of the global SFC matrix. Per build/audit/dp3_identifiability.md, CPIS RESTORES the dense bilateral structure F3/F4 need for global identification.

bilateral_or_marginals.determination: BILATERAL (full from-whom-to-whom cells, not just marginals).
bilateral_or_marginals.evidence: Live single-cell key USA.A.P_TOTINV_P_USD.S1.S1.JPN.S returns a clean bilateral series 2023-S1..2025-S1. A 14x14 load-bearing reporter x counterpart probe populates every off-diagonal cell for the 13 reporting load-bearing economies. Holder-sector x issuer-sector cross-classification is also populated (SECTOR pos 3 x COUNTERPART_SECTOR pos 4).

dimensions_in_order: INDICATOR (pos 2; codelist IMF.STA:CL_PIP_INDICATOR(4.0.0); P_TOTINV_P_USD=total PI positions USD, P_F51_P_USD=equity, P_F3_P_USD=debt securities)
COUNTERPART_COUNTRY (pos 5, issuer/counterpart economy; codelist IMF.STA:CL_PIP_COUNTRY(2.0.0), ISO-3, residency basis)

country_codelist.iso3_confirmation: Enumerated live: USA, JPN, CHN, GBR, HKG, VGB, BEL, LUX, IRL, DEU, FRA, ITA, NLD all PRESENT. Cayman Islands = CYM (NOT 'KYM' — KYM is ABSENT; 'KYM' was a from-memory guess in the probe brief, corrected against the live codelist). Special aggregates present too (e.g. GX031='World Minus 25 Significant Financial Centers', TX983='Not Specified (including Confidential)', TX091='International Organizations').

evidence_excerpts.dsd_self_id: <str:DataStructure ... id="DSD_PIP"> with urn ...DSD_PIP(5.0.0); dimensions parsed: COUNTRY position="0", ACCOUNTING_ENTRY position="1", INDICATOR position="2", SECTOR position="3", COUNTERPART_SECTOR position="4", COUNTERPART_COUNTRY position="5", FREQUENCY position="6", TimeDimension TIME_PERIOD.
evidence_excerpts.country_members: USA: 'United States'; JPN: 'Japan'; CHN: 'China, People's Republic of'; GBR: 'United Kingdom'; HKG: 'Hong Kong SAR...'; VGB: 'British Virgin Islands'; CYM: 'Cayman Islands'; DEU: 'Germany'; FRA: 'France'; ITA: 'Italy'; NLD: 'Netherlands, The'; BEL: 'Belgium'; LUX: 'Luxembourg'; IRL: 'Ireland'.

working_key_example.meaning: US holder, Assets, total PI positions (USD), holder sector S1=Total economy, issuer sector S1, counterpart=JPN, semi-annual.
working_key_example.note: Reproduced live 2026-06-28: US-resident holdings of Japanese-issued portfolio securities, 2025-S1 = 1,344,145,000,000 USD (~$1.344T). Matches the audit.

access.method: IMF SDMX 2.1 REST API at https://api.imf.org/external/sdmx/2.1/ . Confirmed live (DSD, codelist, data queries all HTTP 200). The data.imf.org HTML portal returns 403 to automated fetch (publisher bot protection) — use the machine API.
access.query_efficiency: detail=serieskeysonly for cheap key enumeration; dimensionAtObservation=AllDimensions for flat obs. Single-reporter wild query (e.g. USA.A.P_TOTINV_P_USD....) returns ~26k obs / ~13k bilateral series in one call.

frequency: Semi-annual (S) and annual (A). Semi-annual collection (end-June / end-December) implemented from end-June 2013; end-December back to 2001. FREQUENCY is dimension pos 6.
reporting_lag: ~8.5 months for the semi-annual reference period. Latest available vintage = 2025-S1 (end-June 2025), read directly off live observation TIME_PERIOD values (not from a secondary summary). The end-June 2025 reference period is now DIRECTLY CONFIRMED from API observations (resolves a prior UNVERIFIED item).

coverage.density_summary: 83 of 259 candidate ISO-3 economies report bilaterally as creditors; the typical reporter resolves ~244 of 259 counterparts (median 244; 51 reporters at >=200). DENSE in the counterpart dimension and among the 83 reporters; SPARSE in the reporter dimension (176 valid-as-counterpart non-reporters). All 14 load-bearing economies populate as counterparts; 13 of 14 also report (VGB/British Virgin Islands does NOT report = the one load-bearing reporter hole; HKG reports but freezes some small-counterpart cells at 2021).

known_frictions: HTTP-200-EMPTY TRAP: a syntactically valid but nonexistent codelist member returns HTTP 200 with 0 observations, NOT an error. A wrong member therefore masquerades as 'no data'. Mitigation: validate every dimension member against the live codelist before recording any empty result as a hole.

US-vs-USA BUG (the specific instance of the trap that caused the prior DP2 HOLE verdict): reporter must be ISO-3 'USA'; '2-letter 'US' returns 200/empty. CL_PIP_COUNTRY is ISO-3 throughout. Cayman is 'CYM' not 'KYM'.
Reporter-side sparsity: CPIS is a VOLUNTARY creditor survey. 176 of 259 economies do not report bilaterally (incl. VGB). Their codes are valid only as counterparts. Not a query defect.

Vintage staleness per cell: individual reporters can drop/freeze specific bilateral cells across vintages (e.g. HKG->BEL, HKG->ITA last reported 2021). The cell exists but has no recent obs; probe a wide period window before declaring a hole.
Residency basis (counterpart = residence of issuer), NOT nationality — see residency_vs_nationality_caveat below.

Reported entries are primarily ASSETS (holder side). Liability/mirror exposures come as 'derived using counterparty information' (SCC) variants; FWTW balancing must handle the asset-vs-derived-liability asymmetry explicitly.
Semi-annual frequency — temporal disaggregation needed for quarterly calendar alignment.
data.imf.org HTML pages return HTTP 403 to automated fetch; use api.imf.org SDMX.
Dataset renamed from 'CPIS' to 'Portfolio Investment Positions by Counterpart Economy' and migrated to SDMX id IMF.STA:PIP(5.0.0)/DSD_PIP; legacy CPIS endpoints/IDs no longer apply.

residency_vs_nationality_caveat.statement: CPIS counterpart (COUNTERPART_COUNTRY) is RESIDENCY-based. CPIS RESTORES the dense bilateral structure F3/F4 need for global identification (per build/audit/dp3_identifiability.md), BUT it does NOT close the offshore-SPV ISSUER-NATIONALITY gap that BIS CBS only narrowed.
residency_vs_nationality_caveat.two_distinct_holes: These are TWO DIFFERENT HOLES. (1) The bilateral-structure hole is CLOSED by CPIS (dense from-whom-to-whom on residency). (2) The issuer-nationality hole is NOT closed: Chinese securities issued via offshore (Cayman/CYM, BVI/VGB) SPVs appear under the SPV's RESIDENCE in CPIS (CYM/VGB), not China's nationality. Evidence in this data: US->CYM 2025-S1 = $2,798B is the single largest US load-bearing counterpart, far exceeding US->CHN = $255B, consistent with large offshore-SPV issuance routed through Cayman residence.
residency_vs_nationality_caveat.carry_forward: The issuer-nationality gap travels forward into DP3 as a BOUNDED, EXPLICITLY DOCUMENTED hole. CPIS MUST NOT be recorded as closing it.
residency_vs_nationality_caveat.must_not: Do not record CPIS as closing the offshore-SPV issuer-nationality gap.

tier_basis: Dataflow id, DSD, 8-dimension order, and ISO-3 country codelist all fetched live 2026-06-28 (HTTP 200) and parsed, not recalled. Working bilateral key reproduced live with real obs. Coverage census run programmatically over all 259 candidate reporters + a 14x14 load-bearing cell probe, with every empty cell either confirmed valid (genuine hole) or resolved (transient/stale). Latest vintage 2025-S1 read directly off observations. The one residual caveat (issuer nationality) is a documented structural limitation, not an unverified field.
unverified: Exact publication lag distribution across reporters (only the aggregate latest vintage 2025-S1 was confirmed from observations; per-reporter publication dates not individually enumerated).
Downloadable file formats from the data.imf.org web UI (CSV/Excel/Add-in) — portal HTML returns HTTP 403, not fetchable; only the SDMX API path is verified.

Complete enumeration of which specific small bilateral cells are confidentiality-suppressed vs simply not reported (a sample of stale cells was identified; the full suppression list was not enumerated).
Whether the 176 non-reporting economies include any that report only liabilities/derived (SCC) but not assets — the census probed the assets/holder side (ACCOUNTING_ENTRY=A) only.
